lines.text: We're {{ELAPSED}} in and the East Side is — Caroline, what are we looking at out there?
Quiet. Abnormally quiet. Wind's picked up to 18 mph sustained, gusts to 24. Visibility dropped to 3 miles. People are staying *indoors*.

Kill count is at 82 cumulative. Last year at this hour we were at 104. That's a 21% variance below trend, which — if I'm reading the historical data correctly — suggests either strategic pause or weather suppression we didn't anticipate.
Strategic pause. Jesus. You mean organized. You mean someone's coordinating out there.
I mean the math doesn't lie. Either they're waiting, or they're cold, or something else happened we don't have visibility on yet.
The silence is the problem. Last year at 01:00 we had reports from the Beltline, State Street, the whole Capitol perimeter was active. Tonight — nothing. No calls. No emergency broadcasts.
The silence feels worse than the noise, Caroline. At least noise means chaos. Silence means someone's thinking.

If we apply the 2167 cold-front model to tonight's atmospheric conditions, we should see activity resumption around 02:30 when the temperature stabilizes. But that assumes normal behavioral patterns, and I'm starting to think tonight isn't following the model.
It's not. Something's different. I can feel it.

tickerItems.text: Madison-wide cumulative: 82 kills. 21% below {{ELAPSED}}-mark YoY. Trend analysis: ANOMALOUS.
East Side reporting minimal activity. Capitol Square perimeter: no breach attempts. Beltline corridor: silent. Scanner traffic: near-zero.
Weather advisory: NW winds 18 sustained, gusts to 24 mph. Visibility 3 miles. Cold front arrival on schedule. Hypothermia risk increasing.
UNUSUAL: No field reports incoming from known hot zones. Tucker's last position update was 00:47 from near Yahara Bridge. No contact since.
